seo.title: Ensuring Output Consistency in AI Agents in Instructor Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ensuring output consistency in ai agents in Instructor agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Instructor, ensuring output consistency in AI agents fails when the patched client.chat.completions.create() cannot enforce Pydantic schema validation despite retries. Agents produce inconsistent structured outputs due to model hallucinations of invalid enum values, partial field extraction, or malformed nested objects. These failures exhaust Instructor's validation retries, causing unhandled exceptions and breaking agent workflows.

howItManifests.symptom: Instructor raises ValidationError after max_retries despite partial_match_strategy='drop' or 'keep'
howItManifests.impact: Agent crashes with unrecoverable BindError, halting entire workflow

howItManifests.symptom: Pydantic model returns with None values for required fields despite mode='json'
howItManifests.impact: Downstream agent logic fails due to missing critical data

howItManifests.scenario: Model hallucinating enum values outside schema
howItManifests.symptom: Instructor extracts invalid enum values like 'INVALID_STATUS' when schema defines ['pending', 'completed']
howItManifests.impact: Silent failures where data passes validation but breaks business logic

howItManifests.scenario: Nested object extraction producing inconsistent depth
howItManifests.symptom: Nested Pydantic models return inconsistent structures across calls (sometimes missing sub-objects)
howItManifests.impact: Agent state becomes corrupted, causing cascading failures

detection.name: Retry Exhaustion Monitor
detection.description: Detects when Instructor's validation retries are exhausted by catching BindError with retry metadata

detection.name: Enum Hallucination Detector
detection.description: Validates extracted enum values against schema to catch hallucinations

detection.name: Nested Structure Consistency Check
detection.description: Verifies nested Pydantic objects have consistent depth across multiple calls
detection.code:
from typing import List
from pydantic import BaseModel

class NestedData(BaseModel):
    items: List[dict]

    def is_consistent(self, other: 'NestedData') -> bool:
        return (len(self.items) == len(other.items) and 
                all(len(item) == len(other.items[i]) for i, item in enumerate(self.items)))

# Detect inconsistency
results = []
for _ in range(3):
    result = client.messages.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o-mini',
        response_model=NestedData,
        messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Extract nested data'}]
    )
    results.append(result)

consistent = all(results[0].is_consistent(r) for r in results[1:])
if not consistent:
    print('Nested structure inconsistency detected')

fixes.name: Robust Retry with Fallback Schema
fixes.description: Uses partial=True and custom error handling to prevent retry exhaustion
fixes.code:
from instructor import Instructor
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional

class User(BaseModel):
    name: str = Field(..., description='User full name')
    age: Optional[int] = Field(None, ge=0)

client = Instructor(client=OpenAI())

@client.retry_on(failure_type=ValueError, max_retries=2)
def safe_extract():
    return client.messages.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o-mini',
        response_model=User,
        messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Extract: John, 30 years old'}],
        partial=True,
        mode='json',
        temperature=0.1
    )

fixes.name: Strict Enum Coercion
fixes.description: Forces model to respect enum values using constrained prompt and validation
fixes.code:
from enum import Enum
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class Status(str, Enum):
    PENDING = 'pending'
    COMPLETED = 'completed'

class Task(BaseModel):
    status: Status = Field(..., description="Must be 'pending' or 'completed'")

response = client.messages.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    response_model=Task,
    messages=[
        {'role': 'system', 'content': "Status must be exactly 'pending' or 'completed'. No other values."},
        {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Task is pending completion'}
    ],
    temperature=0,
    max_retries=1
)

fixes.name: Nested Validation with Default Structure
fixes.description: Provides default nested structure to ensure consistent depth
fixes.code:
from typing import List, Dict, Any
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class NestedItem(BaseModel):
    id: str
    value: str

class ConsistentResponse(BaseModel):
    items: List[NestedItem] = Field(default_factory=list, max_items=10)
    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)

response = client.messages.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    response_model=ConsistentResponse,
    messages=[
        {'role': 'system', 'content': 'Always return items as array of objects with id and value fields.'},
        {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Extract structured data'}
    ],
    partial=True
)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Ensuring Output Consistency in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automatically wraps Instructor's client.chat.completions.create() calls with vex.verify(), providing runtime monitoring of validation failures, retry exhaustion, and schema violations. It captures detailed telemetry on Pydantic validation errors, tracks hallucinated enum values, and measures nested structure consistency across agent runs. Vex generates automated test cases from failure patterns and suggests optimal Instructor configuration parameters like max_retries, temperature, and partial_match_strategy.

The platform maintains a failure baseline for your Instructor agents, alerting on regression in output consistency. When issues are detected, Vex applies progressive fixes - from prompt engineering to schema coercion - and validates remediation through A/B testing. Integration requires wrapping your existing Instructor client with a single vex.verify() decorator.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex import verify
from instructor import Instructor
from openai import OpenAI
from pydantic import BaseModel

# Install: pip install instructor vex-sdk
client = Instructor(client=OpenAI())

@verify(test_name='instructor-consistency-check')
def extract_user_data(prompt: str):
    return client.messages.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o-mini',
        response_model=User,
        messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': prompt}],
        max_retries=3,
        temperature=0.1
    )

# Vex automatically detects:
# - Validation retry exhaustion
# - Enum hallucinations
# - Partial field extraction
# - Nested structure inconsistency

result = extract_user_data('Extract user: John Doe, age 30')
